Flashback example index / corpus 2024

2024

2024: the first draft is automated, but trust is not.

Spatial fatigue

Spatial fatigue — headset futures, prompt gloss, and human grain push against one another.

  • Climate
  • Recipes
  • Corpus map
  • Prompt seeds
  • Full corpus

Jump nearby

2019 Mono hipster 2020 Remote shock 2021 Spec surf 2022 Prompt shock 2023 Human texture 2024 Spatial fatigue 2025 Glass grit 2026 Adaptive bloom

Climate

2024 is pulled between generated polish and human residue.

01

Spatial interface grammar: windows in depth, eye-and-hand interaction, persona video, pass-through environments, and the question of how UI behaves when it floats

02

AI as co-worker and contaminant: assistants speed drafts, but plagiarism, provenance, bias, and sameness become part of the design brief

03

Bento-box information architecture: dashboards and launches use modular rounded tiles to make complex products feel digestible

04

Raw-web reaction: neo-brutalist borders, clashing colors, default controls, chunky buttons, and unpretty honesty push back against SaaS smoothness

05

Variable and kinetic type: big flexible type carries identity across responsive, social, and motion contexts

06

Olympic heritage remix: Paris 2024 uses Art Deco cues, a Marianne/flame/medal emblem, and badge-like pictograms to make a global event feel local

07

Apple Vision Pro is released in the United States on February 2

08

Figma announces AI features, UI3, and Figma Slides at Config 2024

Example recipes

Generated from the current Flashback design recipes in the 2024 corpus.

Recipe 01

Spatial luxury interface

Use for: immersive products, premium hardware, productivity tools, medical visualization, museum experiences.

Palette
black glass, warm off-white, violet, cyan, soft grey.
Type
clean geometric sans with restrained mono labels and large calm headings.
Layout
floating panels, layered depth, bento modules, generous air, centered focus.
Imagery
hands, rooms, translucent windows, eye-level views, objects at real scale.
Motion
parallax, soft dock, subtle depth shift, hand-triggered expansion.

Risk: making everything look like a headset keynote.
Accuracy: interaction feedback, comfort, and spatial hierarchy rather than random glass.

 Recipe 02

AI provenance lab

Use for: creative tools, archives, trust products, editorial explainers, model interfaces.

Palette
off-white, ink black, muted blue, warning amber, terminal green.
Type
readable sans, monospaced metadata, clear attribution labels.
Layout
split view, prompt/result/history, source cards, visible versioning.
Imagery
drafts, masks, fingerprints, screenshots, citations, before-and-after states.
Motion
generation reveal, comparison scrub, audit trail unfolding.

Risk: celebrating AI magic while hiding responsibility.
Accuracy: provenance, constraints, and human decision points.

 Recipe 03

Raw bento web

Use for: startups, cultural sites, portfolios, tools, newsletters, creative communities.

Palette
black, paper, acid violet, turquoise, hot pink.
Type
oversized variable display, system sans body, terminal captions.
Layout
modular tiles with hard edges, uneven rhythm, visible rules, dense cards.
Imagery
screenshots, scans, phone photos, grainy crops, UI fragments.
Motion
snap, drag, shuffle, abrupt hover states, cursor-led reveals.

Risk: mistaking ugliness for honesty.
Accuracy: real information architecture under the rough surface.

 Recipe 04

Paris civic spectacle

Use for: sports, public events, cultural festivals, city campaigns, broadcast packages.

Palette
cream, black, gold, lavender, red, blue, green accents.
Type
heritage display paired with crisp wayfinding sans.
Layout
badge systems, vertical banners, medal-like emblems, broadcast-safe grids.
Imagery
landmarks, athletes, pictogram badges, Art Deco geometry, crowd color.
Motion
torch glow, ribbon sweep, scoreboard snap, ceremony pacing.

Risk: generic French postcard nostalgia.
Accuracy: real event-system logic: wayfinding, pictograms, ceremonies, venues.

Corpus map

Every card links to a live heading in the source corpus.

  • Year thesisthe first draft is automated, but trust is not
  • 2024 to 2023Year-to-year change.
  • Design climate2024 is pulled between generated polish and human residue.
  • Timeline signalsApple Vision Pro is released in the United States on February 2, Figma announces AI feature...
  • Typography2024 typography is split between system clarity, expressive scale, and proof of authorship.
  • Graphic design2024 graphic design is a contest between the generated image and the authored system.
  • Product design2024 product design asks whether AI needs an object.
  • Architecture2024 interiors are shaped by hybrid work, hospitality warmth, and screen-aware rooms.
  • Fashion2024 self-design is hyper-mediated and authenticity-hungry.
  • Music2024 music design is fast, public, and highly graphic.
  • Film2024 moving image is dominated by scale, texture, and AI anxiety.
  • Surface2024 surfaces oscillate between soft comfort and acid interface light.
  • Anti-clichesDo not make 2024 look like:

Prompt seeds

Ready-to-run prompts pulled from the corpus.

Design this through a 2024 lens: Apple Vision Pro has made spatial computing
visible, Figma has put AI inside the design workflow, and designers are pushing
back against generic generated polish with grain, raw grids, and provenance.
Keep the tension between automation and authorship clear.
Give me three 2024-informed directions:
1. Spatial luxury interface
2. AI provenance lab
3. Raw bento web
For each, explain the typography, color, interaction model, material cues, and
what to avoid.
Critique this layout as if it shipped in 2024. Does it feel like a trustworthy
AI-era design system, a spatial-computing demo, a Paris civic spectacle, or a
neo-brutalist reaction against smooth SaaS?

Reference artifacts

Objects, graphics, and spaces that anchor the year.

Objects

  • Apple Vision Pro
  • Humane AI Pin
  • Rabbit R1
  • iPhone running Arc Search
  • Headset bands, light seals, passthrough cameras, and hand-tracking gestures
  • AI prompt fields, provenance panels, and generated UI drafts

Print and graphics

  • Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic identity, pictograms, and wayfinding
  • Figma Config 2024 AI and UI3 launch materials
  • Pantone Color of the Year 2024: Peach Fuzz
  • Dune: Part Two posters, costume stills, and production design
  • Kendrick Lamar "Not Like Us" cover and meme circulation
  • Neo-brutalist websites with hard borders, clashing colors, and system type

Spaces

  • Apple Vision Pro demo rooms and domestic passthrough environments
  • Paris 2024 venues, ceremonies, fan zones, and wayfinding corridors
  • Hybrid offices built around video calls and collaboration areas
  • Home workspaces with lights, cameras, monitors, plants, and acoustic treatment
  • AI product launch stages and conference demo screens

Anti-cliches

Guardrails from the corpus to keep the year specific.

  • Generic AI fantasy art with glossy faces and no concept
  • Apple glass copied without interaction logic
  • A pile of bento cards with no hierarchy
  • Cyberpunk neon pretending to be current AI culture
  • Neo-brutalism used as random bad taste
  • Olympic design reduced to flags and Eiffel Tower icons
  • Peach softness with no technological tension
  • Handmade texture used as a filter rather than evidence of process
24

2024 rule: the first draft is automated, but trust is not.
